title: System Failure
description: A primary subsystem has failed mid-voyage. Engineering wants parts and power; the council wants a plan.

outcomes.label: Emergency repair
outcomes.description: Spend minerals and credits to restore the system fully.
outcomes.log: Engineering crews restored the failed system at material cost.

outcomes.label: Reroute power
outcomes.description: Bypass the failure. Cheap now, a debt for later.
outcomes.log: Power was rerouted around the failure. The bypass will outlive its welcome.

title: Population Growth
description: A healthy cohort of births strains food planning but promises a stronger next generation.

outcomes.label: Expand rations
outcomes.description: Feed the boom.
outcomes.log: The ship's population grew, and the galley budget grew with it.

outcomes.label: Hold rations steady
outcomes.description: No new allocations. The nursery decks will remember.
outcomes.log: Rations held steady through the birth cohort. Some decks kept score.

title: Arrival Burn
description: The destination system resolves from a bright dot into a place. Final approach requires a heavy deceleration burn.

outcomes.label: Textbook burn
outcomes.description: By the book, on the fuel budget.
outcomes.log: The arrival burn went by the book. The observation decks were crowded for a week.

title: Cultural Schism
description: Two decks now keep different calendars. The disagreement has stopped being polite.

outcomes.label: Council mediation
outcomes.description: Spend influence to reconcile the factions.
outcomes.log: The council brokered a settlement between the divided decks.

outcomes.label: Let it run its course
outcomes.description: Cultures drift. Intervening has costs too.
outcomes.log: The schism was left to run its course. One faction would not be reconciled; they took a decommissioned shuttle and their grievances, and departed on their own course.

title: Micrometeoroid Storm
description: A debris front the forward sensors caught too late. The hull is taking hits and the shelters are filling.

outcomes.label: Brace and seal
outcomes.description: Shutter the outer decks and ride it out. Slow, safe, hard on nerves.
outcomes.log: The ship shuttered its outer decks and rode the storm out, scarred but whole.

outcomes.label: Burn around it
outcomes.description: Spend fuel to steer clear. The schedule slips; the hull survives.
outcomes.log: A hard burn carried the ship clear of the debris front, at the cost of the fuel budget.

title: Coolant Breach
description: A coolant line has ruptured in the reactor annex. Every minute of delay is a degree the core will not forgive.

outcomes.label: Vent the annex
outcomes.description: Blow the section to space. The core is saved; the annex crew are not all accounted for.
outcomes.log: The annex was vented to save the core. The memorial wall grew by a column.

outcomes.label: Order a manual patch
outcomes.description: Send engineers in with what they have. Expensive, and not without risk.
outcomes.log: Engineers patched the breach by hand. The reactor runs, and runs hot.

title: The Skills Drought
description: The last founding specialists are retiring faster than apprentices can replace them. Institutional memory is thinning.

outcomes.label: Crash apprenticeship
outcomes.description: Pull hands from other duties to train replacements now.
outcomes.log: A crash apprenticeship filled the benches, if not yet the shoes.

outcomes.label: Lean on the records
outcomes.description: Trust the archives to carry what the elders knew. Some of it will not translate.
outcomes.log: The crews leaned on the archives. Something in the doing, not the knowing, was lost.

title: The Restless Generation
description: A cohort born to the corridors has decided the mission is their grandparents' promise, not theirs.

outcomes.label: Seat them on the council
outcomes.description: Fold the dissent into the institution. It changes both.
outcomes.log: The restless generation was given a seat and a stake. The council sounds younger now.

outcomes.label: Hold the founders' line
outcomes.description: The charter is not up for renegotiation. Say so plainly.
outcomes.log: The council held the founders' line. The corridors went quiet in a way that keeps.

title: The Halfway Beacon
description: The ship passes the midpoint marker its founders laid in the plan. Half the voyage is behind; no one living chose the far half.

outcomes.label: Mark the crossing
outcomes.description: A day of ceremony and rest. Morale answers.
outcomes.log: The ship marked the halfway crossing. For a day, the corridors felt like a procession — and the founders had left more than a marker: a ramscoop module, cached at the beacon, now folded into the salvage hold.

title: A Silent Hull
description: Sensors snag a derelict on the transit lane — an older ship, dark, drifting. It answers no hail.

outcomes.label: Board and salvage
outcomes.description: Take what the dead ship offers. Not everyone approves.
outcomes.log: The boarding parties stripped the derelict for parts — and cut a working mass driver free of its spine, now lashed in the salvage hold.

outcomes.label: Log it and pass
outcomes.description: Record the position, say the words, keep the schedule.
outcomes.log: The ship logged the derelict's position, spoke for its dead, and passed on.

title: The Founders' Creed
description: A movement wants the founding charter read aloud each year, word for word, forever. Another wants it revised for a ship the founders never saw.

outcomes.label: Enshrine the charter
outcomes.description: The founders' words become law and liturgy both.
outcomes.log: The charter was enshrined, unaltered. The ship knows exactly who it was meant to be.

outcomes.label: Amend the charter
outcomes.description: A living document for a living ship. Every word changed is a small funeral.
outcomes.log: The charter was amended. The founders would not recognize the ship; the ship is not sure it minds.

title: The Right to Name
description: The destination needs a name for the charts and the histories. Every faction has one ready, and a reason it must win.

outcomes.label: Honor the founders' name
outcomes.description: The name they wrote into the plan, kept.
outcomes.log: The destination kept the name its founders chose. Some traditions are only words until you keep them.

outcomes.label: Let the ship choose
outcomes.description: A vote, deck by deck. Messy, and theirs.
outcomes.log: The ship named its own destination by vote. The charts bear a word no founder wrote.

title: The Slow Crack
description: A hairline fracture is walking its way across a spine frame. It is not urgent yet. 'Yet' is doing a great deal of work in that sentence.

outcomes.label: Reinforce it now
outcomes.description: Pull minerals and crews to brace the frame before it spreads.
outcomes.log: The spine frame was braced before the crack could vote on the matter.

outcomes.label: Monitor and defer
outcomes.description: Sensor it, log it, spend nothing. A problem for a later council.
outcomes.log: The crack was catalogued and left to grow. It will keep its appointment.

title: Recycled Air
description: Something is moving through the ventilation the way rumors move through the decks. The medics want the decks sealed; the foremen want them working.

outcomes.label: Seal the decks
outcomes.description: Lose the labor, keep the lungs.
outcomes.log: The decks were sealed and the outbreak starved of hosts. Production limped; the ward filled slowly.

outcomes.label: Work through it
outcomes.description: Keep the ship moving. The strong will carry the weak, or not.
outcomes.log: The ship worked through the sickness. The memorial wall did the accounting.

title: The Thin Cohort
description: This generation came small. The nurseries have empty cribs and the actuaries have furrowed brows.

outcomes.label: Incentivize births
outcomes.description: Spend influence and rations to fill the cribs.
outcomes.log: The council's incentives filled the nurseries. The next census will thank them.

outcomes.label: Accept a lean generation
outcomes.description: A smaller crew eats less and argues less. It also builds less.
outcomes.log: The lean cohort grew up close-knit and few. The ship ran quieter, and shorter-handed.

title: The Crowded Classroom
description: The school decks were built for a smaller ship. The waiting lists are longer than the curriculum.

outcomes.label: Expand the schools
outcomes.description: Convert deck space and credits into classrooms.
outcomes.log: New classrooms opened on a converted cargo deck. A generation will be better taught for it.

title: The Fork in the Dark
description: Two viable courses to the destination diverge here: a fast lane through a debris shelf, or a slow safe arc. The nav computer wants a decision it cannot make.

outcomes.label: Take the fast lane
outcomes.description: Shave years off the crossing. Bet the hull on good sensors.
outcomes.log: The ship threaded the debris shelf and gained a decade. The hull wears the toll.

outcomes.label: Take the safe arc
outcomes.description: Add years, keep the plating.
outcomes.log: The ship took the long safe arc. The schedule slipped; nothing else did.

title: A Voice on the Relay
description: An automated relay, older than the mission, is broadcasting a handshake into the dark. It wants to talk. It may only be an echo.

outcomes.label: Answer the relay
outcomes.description: Trade data with the old machine. Knowledge, and exposure.
outcomes.log: The ship answered the relay and came away with charts and a strange new vocabulary.

outcomes.label: Run silent
outcomes.description: Log its position, tell it nothing, keep the ship's shape a secret.
outcomes.log: The ship passed the relay in silence. Whatever it wanted, it will keep wanting.

title: The First Rite
description: A generation born entirely in transit must decide how to bury its dead — the founders' soil rites, impossible here, or something the ship invents for itself.

outcomes.label: Keep the soil rite
outcomes.description: Use precious hydroponic loam for symbolic burial. Costly, and rooted.
outcomes.log: The dead were laid in borrowed soil, as the founders would know. The farms felt the loss.

outcomes.label: Invent a ship rite
outcomes.description: Commit the dead to the void with new words. Cheaper, and untethered.
outcomes.log: The ship spoke new words over its first spacers' dead. A tradition began that no founder wrote.

title: The Last Founders
description: The final three crew who were awake at departure petition the council: they want the mission's original wording read into the record before they die, unamended.

outcomes.label: Honor the petition
outcomes.description: Read the founding words as written. Let the old crew hear them once more.
outcomes.log: The founding words were read whole to the last who remembered them. Some wept; none objected.

outcomes.label: Read the current charter
outcomes.description: The living ship's amended wording is the true text now. Honesty over sentiment.
outcomes.log: The amended charter was read instead. The last founders heard a mission they did not entirely recognize.

title: Reactor Scram
description: The core dropped its rods on an automatic fault. The ship runs on batteries now, and everyone can hear the silence where the hum should be.

outcomes.label: Cold restart by the book
outcomes.description: Slow, safe, hours in the dark.
outcomes.log: The core came back on a careful cold restart. The dark was long and the coffee ran out.

outcomes.label: Override and hot-restart
outcomes.description: Fast, and the fault might still be there.
outcomes.log: The engineers hot-restarted the core over its own objections. It runs; it grumbles.

outcomes.label: Scram for good — turn back
outcomes.description: The core won't be trusted again. Cut the mission and limp home on what power remains.
outcomes.log: The council will not gamble the ship on a scarred core. The mission is cut short — the long road home begins on battery and prayer.

title: The Tail
description: A ship with no transponder has matched the voyage's heading for three days. It is not closing. It is not leaving.

outcomes.label: Warn it off
outcomes.description: Light it up and make the ship look expensive to attack.
outcomes.log: A show of teeth sent the shadow drifting back into the dark. Probably.

outcomes.label: Hold course, say nothing
outcomes.description: Give it nothing to react to.
outcomes.log: The ship held course and the tail eventually fell away. The observation decks watched the whole time.

title: The Thickening Air
description: Two CO2 scrubbers failed in the same week. The air is going stale deck by deck and the spares are a generation old.

outcomes.label: Cannibalize for parts
outcomes.description: Strip other systems to rebuild the scrubbers.
outcomes.log: The scrubbers breathe again, assembled from the corpses of lesser machines.

outcomes.label: Ration activity
outcomes.description: Slow the ship to slow the breathing.
outcomes.log: The ship moved at half-effort until the air cleared. Everyone learned to sit very still.

title: The Idle Hands
description: Automation has outrun the population. A generation has grown up with too little to do and too much time to wonder why they are here.

outcomes.label: Fund make-work projects
outcomes.description: Spend credits on purpose, even invented purpose.
outcomes.log: The council funded a hundred small projects. Idle hands found rails to paint and songs to write.

outcomes.label: Let them find their own purpose
outcomes.description: Trust boredom to breed either art or trouble.
outcomes.log: Left to themselves, the idle generation invented new things to be, some of them useful.

title: Too Many Hands
description: This cohort trained hard for posts that are already filled. The waiting list for real work is longer than a career.

outcomes.label: Rotate the posts
outcomes.description: Share the work thin so everyone gets some.
outcomes.log: The posts were rotated so the trained could stay sharp. Nobody is idle; nobody is expert.

title: The Slingshot
description: A massive body sits just off the course. A gravity assist could bank years of fuel — if the approach is flawless.

outcomes.label: Thread the slingshot
outcomes.description: Fly close, gain speed, risk the tides.
outcomes.log: The ship whipped past the giant and came out faster and lighter on fuel. The tides only rattled the crockery.

outcomes.label: Skip the maneuver
outcomes.description: Burn the fuel, keep the plating.
outcomes.log: The council skipped the slingshot and paid in fuel for the peace of mind.

title: The Dark Stretch
description: Ahead lies a region the founders' charts simply stop describing — a long stretch where the sensors will have little to say.

outcomes.label: Run it steady
outcomes.description: Trust the plan and the dead reckoning.
outcomes.log: The ship crossed the dark stretch on dead reckoning and old faith. It came out where the charts said it would.

title: The Cache
description: A supply cache the mission planners seeded decades ago drifts on the course ahead, if its beacon can be trusted.

outcomes.label: Match and claim it
outcomes.description: Spend fuel to rendezvous with the drop.
outcomes.log: The cache gave up rations and metals — and, folded in its heart, an intact solar sail array, stowed in the salvage hold.

outcomes.label: Pass it by
outcomes.description: Stay on the fuel budget; the holds are full enough.
outcomes.log: The cache drifted past, unopened. The ship needs nothing yet, and the founders keep their gift.

outcomes.label: A fortune — sail home on it
outcomes.description: Far richer than charted. With holds full and a windfall aboard, the charter is as good as met.
outcomes.log: The cache was no mere resupply but a mother lode — metals and salvage worth more than the whole charter. The council turns the ship for home, holds heavy with fortune.

title: Born to the Corridors
description: For the first time, the dynasty's leader was born, raised, and will die aboard — with no memory, even inherited, of a world with a sky.

outcomes.label: Mark the moment
outcomes.description: Acknowledge that the ship now leads itself, from itself.
outcomes.log: The ship marked its first wholly ship-born leader. Something ended quietly; something began louder.

title: The Memory Wall
description: The memorial wall has run out of room. The council must decide whether to extend it, archive the oldest names, or begin again.

outcomes.label: Extend the wall
outcomes.description: Give the dead more room, whatever it costs the living decks.
outcomes.log: The memorial wall grew a new wing. No name will be moved while the ship still flies.

outcomes.label: Archive the oldest names
outcomes.description: Move the earliest dead into the record to make room.
outcomes.log: The oldest names went to the archive to make room for the newly gone. The founders moved over, a little.

title: The Snare
description: The ship has run into a lattice of old orbital debris — cables, panels, the bones of something that failed here. It is fouling the forward sensors and worse.

outcomes.label: Cut free
outcomes.description: Send crews out to clear it by hand. Slow, and exposed.
outcomes.log: The debris lattice was cut away over three shifts of dangerous work. The forward array sees again — and the crews dragged back a slab of reinforced prow-plate, too big to fit in the field but worth stowing for the next drydock.

outcomes.label: Power through
outcomes.description: Push forward and let the hull take what it takes.
outcomes.log: The ship shouldered through the snare and came out the other side scored and free.

title: The Cascade
description: One tripped breaker became ten. Half the ship is dark and the load-balancing computer is asking the council a question it was built to answer itself.

outcomes.label: Shed the load
outcomes.description: Cut non-essential decks to save the core grid.
outcomes.log: Whole decks went dark to save the grid. People learned which of their comforts were negotiable.

outcomes.label: Rebalance by hand
outcomes.description: Spend parts and hours coaxing the grid back whole.
outcomes.log: Engineers rebalanced the grid by hand, deck by deck, until the lights came back on together.

title: The Prodigy
description: A child in the new cohort reads the ship's systems the way others read faces. The council can fast-track them, or let them grow up ordinary.

outcomes.label: Fast-track the prodigy
outcomes.description: Give them every system and every hour.
outcomes.log: The prodigy was given the run of the ship's systems. In a decade the ship will be glad; the cohort is less sure now.

outcomes.label: Let them be a child
outcomes.description: No cohort should carry the ship on one set of shoulders.
outcomes.log: The council let the prodigy grow up ordinary. The ship kept its gift in reserve, and its childhood intact.

title: The Debt Comes Due
description: The maintenance the founders deferred and the last three generations ignored has all come due in the same decade. Something must be chosen, and something let go.

outcomes.label: Fund a full overhaul
outcomes.description: Pay the whole debt at once. It hurts now or later.
outcomes.log: The ship paid its inherited maintenance debt in full. The treasury is lighter; the ship will outlast the council that paid.

outcomes.label: Patch what fails
outcomes.description: Fix only what breaks. Cheaper, and a wager.
outcomes.log: The ship patched only what failed and left the rest to the next generation, as had every generation before.

title: First Light
description: For the first time, the destination star can be picked out with the naked eye from the observation deck — a specific point of light, not a coordinate.

outcomes.label: Gather and look
outcomes.description: Call the ship to the windows.
outcomes.log: The ship crowded the observation decks to see its destination as a star. Some had waited their whole lives to look.

title: The Final Approach
description: The founders wrote a final-approach plan for a ship they never saw finished. The council can ratify it as written or amend it with everything the voyage has learned.

outcomes.label: Ratify as written
outcomes.description: Trust the plan the founders sealed.
outcomes.log: The founders' approach plan was ratified unchanged. The ship will arrive exactly as it was told to.

outcomes.label: Amend with what we know
outcomes.description: The voyage taught things the founders could not know.
outcomes.log: The approach plan was amended with a lifetime of hard-won data. The founders would not recognize it; it is better.

title: A Voice From Home
description: A tight-beam message from the origin world arrives, decades stale — routine, official, and addressed to a ship the senders assume is still the one that departed.

outcomes.label: Answer home
outcomes.description: Send a reply into a fifty-year silence.
outcomes.log: The ship answered the old signal from home. The reply will arrive to a world that may no longer be listening.

outcomes.label: Log it and move on
outcomes.description: Home is a place none of them will see. Face forward.
outcomes.log: The message from home was logged and archived, unanswered. The ship's home is the ship now.

title: The Last Sky
description: The last surviving photograph of an open planetary sky — the founders' sky — has degraded to almost nothing. The archivists ask whether to restore it, or let it finish fading.

outcomes.label: Restore the image
outcomes.description: Spend to keep the founders' sky.
outcomes.log: The last sky was painstakingly restored. A ship born to corridors keeps one window onto the world it left.

outcomes.label: Let it fade
outcomes.description: Some things are meant to be let go.
outcomes.log: The last photograph of an open sky was allowed to finish fading. The ship's children will never miss what they cannot picture.

title: The Freeze
description: A coolant line froze and split where the ship runs coldest. If the crack spreads to the main loop, a whole section goes dark.

outcomes.label: Bleed the line
outcomes.description: Purge and reroute before it spreads. Costs power and hours.
outcomes.log: The frozen line was bled and rerouted before the crack could reach the main loop. Cold work, cleanly done.

outcomes.label: Seal the section
outcomes.description: Close it off and lose the deck rather than risk the loop.
outcomes.log: The frozen section was sealed off and abandoned. The main loop is safe; the ship is one deck smaller.

title: The Bloom
description: Something is growing in the water reclamation tanks that the ship's biology was not designed to host. It is spreading faster than the filters can name it.

outcomes.label: Sterilize the tanks
outcomes.description: Flush and burn the system clean. Wasteful, and thorough.
outcomes.log: The reclamation tanks were sterilized to bare metal. The bloom is gone, and so is a season's margin of water and food.

outcomes.label: Quarantine and study
outcomes.description: Isolate it and hope the filters catch up. Cheaper, and a gamble.
outcomes.log: The bloom was quarantined and studied rather than burned out. It is contained, for now, and the crew drink boiled water and worry.

title: The Slowdown
description: The maintenance cohort has stopped working to the letter of a contract the founders wrote for a different ship. Nothing is broken yet. Everything is slower.

outcomes.label: Concede the terms
outcomes.description: Rewrite the cohort's terms in their favor.
outcomes.log: The council conceded the cohort's terms. The work resumed at full speed, and every cohort now knows the council can be moved.

outcomes.label: Hold the line
outcomes.description: The contract stands. Wait them out.
outcomes.log: The council held the founders' terms. The slowdown ended in sullen compliance, and something between the decks did not fully mend.

title: The Berth Lottery
description: This generation was born larger than the last, and there are more people than private berths. Someone decides who sleeps in the open decks.

outcomes.label: Expand the quarters
outcomes.description: Convert cargo and hold space into homes.
outcomes.log: Cargo space was cut open and rebuilt into berths. The ship carries less and shelters more.

outcomes.label: Run the lottery
outcomes.description: Assign the open decks by lot. Fair, and resented.
outcomes.log: Private berths were assigned by lottery. The ship called it fair, and the losers of the draw learned to sleep in the light.

outcomes.label: Let a people take the settlement offer
outcomes.description: A passing survey world will take a whole faction. Crowding eases; the ship is smaller for it.
outcomes.log: One people accepted a standing offer to make planetfall on a surveyed world. The berths are roomier now, and a light aboard has gone out for good.

title: The Scout Returns
description: A probe the founders flung ahead a lifetime ago has finally answered — a thin stream of data about the place the ship is aimed at. Some of it does not match the charts the voyage was planned around.

outcomes.label: Trust the new data
outcomes.description: Revise the plan around what the probe actually found.
outcomes.log: The probe's data was trusted over the founders' charts, and the approach was quietly rebuilt around a world no one had promised.

outcomes.label: Keep the founders' charts
outcomes.description: One probe is one probe. Trust the plan that got you here.
outcomes.log: The council kept faith with the founders' charts. The probe's warnings were filed, and the ship held the course it was given.

title: The Midpoint Census
description: The ship takes a full census at the mathematical midpoint of the voyage — every name aboard, living and remembered, counted once against the founders' manifest.

outcomes.label: Count them all
outcomes.description: Read the whole manifest aloud.
outcomes.log: The midpoint census was read aloud over three days, every name from the founders' manifest to the newest birth. The ship felt, briefly, like one thing.

title: The Founder's Ledger
description: A founder's private ledger surfaces in the archives — not the official record, but the doubts, the debts, and the compromises behind the mission's clean origin story.

outcomes.label: Publish the ledger
outcomes.description: Let the ship see its founders whole.
outcomes.log: The founder's private ledger was published entire. The founders are smaller now, and truer, and harder to worship.

outcomes.label: Seal the ledger
outcomes.description: Some myths hold a ship together. Let this one stand.
outcomes.log: The ledger was sealed back into the archive. The founders keep their clean story, and the ship keeps the myth it steers by.

title: The Renaming
description: A movement has grown to rename the ship — not for the world it left, which no one aboard remembers, but for the corridors that are the only home its people have known.

outcomes.label: Rename the ship
outcomes.description: Let the ship be named by those who live in it.
outcomes.log: The ship was renamed by the generation that lives in it, over the objections of the archive. The old name is a footnote now, and the corridors answer to their own.

outcomes.label: Keep the founders' name
outcomes.description: The name is a promise to a place. Keep it.
outcomes.log: The founders' name was kept. The ship still carries the word for a world none of its people will ever walk, because the founders asked it to.

title: The Ion Reef
description: A shoal of charged plasma glitters across the bow, beautiful and unmapped. The instruments want a closer look; the pilots want a wide berth.

outcomes.label: Chart it close
outcomes.description: Thread the shoal for the data.
outcomes.log: A week in the glow, and the survey banks a reef no chart has ever held.

outcomes.label: Skirt it wide
outcomes.description: Log it and keep the distance.
outcomes.log: The reef fell astern, unentered. The pilots slept easier for it.

title: The Clock Disagreement
description: Two chronometers, once synchronized on the founding day, no longer agree. The dark has been bending time by fractions no one felt passing.

outcomes.label: Recalibrate to the beacon
outcomes.description: Trust the outside clock.
outcomes.log: The ship reset its calendar against the last known beacon. A handful of days simply vanished from the record.

outcomes.label: Keep ship-time
outcomes.description: The ship's own year is the true one now.
outcomes.log: The council ruled that ship-time is the only time that matters out here. The old calendar became a story.

title: The Dark Body
description: Something crosses the star-field ahead — a mass that gives back no light, only a shadow where the stars should be.

outcomes.label: Range it with active scan
outcomes.description: Paint it and learn what it is.
outcomes.log: The pulse came back strange and slow. Whatever it is, it is old, and it is not natural.

outcomes.label: Run dark past it
outcomes.description: Give it nothing to look back at.
outcomes.log: The ship ran silent until the shadow was well astern. Some questions keep.

title: The Whisper
description: A faint, patient tug on the ship's mass — a gravity that has no visible source. The astronomers argue in the corridors; the priests have already decided.

outcomes.label: Study the whisper
outcomes.description: Turn every instrument on it.
outcomes.log: Months of data, and only deeper questions. The whisper stayed a whisper — but the ship's scholars will chase it for generations.

outcomes.label: Look away
outcomes.description: Some things are not for us to weigh.
outcomes.log: The instruments were turned elsewhere. The whisper went on tugging, unmeasured.

title: The Lobites
description: A hold-stowed pair of lobites — soft, round, endlessly agreeable, and endlessly breeding — has become a hold-filling thousand. They eat little and purr, but there are so very many.

outcomes.label: Cull the warren
outcomes.description: Thin them back to a manageable few.
outcomes.log: The lobites were culled to a sensible number. The children cried; the quartermaster did not.

outcomes.label: Keep them all
outcomes.description: Morale is worth a little spilled grain.
outcomes.log: The ship kept its lobites. The corridors are louder, warmer, and slightly stickier for it.

title: The Inspection
description: A charter clerk, aboard since departure and forgotten by everyone, has emerged to conduct the mandated mid-voyage audit. He has a clipboard. He is very serious.

outcomes.label: Humor the audit
outcomes.description: Give him his forms and his afternoon.
outcomes.log: The clerk filed a forty-page report no one will read. He seemed, at last, content.

outcomes.label: Lose the paperwork
outcomes.description: Regretfully, the archive ate his forms.
outcomes.log: The audit was tragically lost to a filing error. The ship ran on, unaudited and unbothered.

title: Lost in Translation
description: An automated relay greeting, kept running for centuries, has been quietly mistranslating the ship's name as something deeply unflattering to everyone who hears it.

outcomes.label: Rewrite the greeting
outcomes.description: Restore a little dignity.
outcomes.log: The greeting was corrected. Somewhere, a relay operator stopped laughing at last.

title: The Ship's Cat
description: A corridor animal — no one is certain what species — has adopted the bridge crew, the night shift, and, apparently, the entire ship. It answers to no name and every name.

outcomes.label: Make it official
outcomes.description: Enter it in the crew manifest.
outcomes.log: The animal was entered on the manifest with the rank of Morale Officer. It has not reported for duty once, and morale has never been higher.

title: The Convoy Hail
description: A slower ship on a crossing course hails for trade — they have minerals to spare and a hunger for news and seed stock.

outcomes.label: Open the holds
outcomes.description: Deal fairly and part friends.
outcomes.log: Goods and gossip changed hands across the dark. Both ships sailed on the richer for it.

outcomes.label: Wave them off
outcomes.description: Keep to the schedule and the strangers at arm's length.
outcomes.log: The hail went unanswered. The convoy dwindled astern, and the council wondered, later, what news it carried.

title: The Boundary
description: A beacon on the route broadcasts a claim: this stretch of empty dark belongs to a power no one aboard has heard of, and passage requires a toll.

outcomes.label: Pay the toll
outcomes.description: Cheaper than a fight, and faster.
outcomes.log: The toll was paid to a flag no one recognized. The ship passed unmolested, and a little poorer.

outcomes.label: Refuse and press on
outcomes.description: No unseen flag rules the black.
outcomes.log: The ship pressed through the claimed dark, defiant. Nothing came of the threat — this time.

title: The Petitioners
description: A battered craft matches speed and begs to come aboard — a handful of souls fleeing something they will not name.

outcomes.label: Take them aboard
outcomes.description: There is always room for a few more.
outcomes.log: The petitioners came aboard, grateful and guarded. The ship is more crowded, and less alone.

outcomes.label: Turn them away
outcomes.description: The ship cannot carry every stray in the dark.
outcomes.log: The council turned the petitioners away. The craft fell behind, and no one aboard spoke of it at supper.

title: The Gene Ledger
description: The medical council petitions to edit the line of the unborn — to trim the inherited frailties a closed gene-pool breeds. The Preservers call it a desecration.

outcomes.label: Approve the edits
outcomes.description: Spare the children the old sicknesses.
outcomes.log: The ledger was opened. The next cohort will be healthier, and something old and unrepeatable was closed forever.

outcomes.label: Forbid the edits
outcomes.description: The line is the line, flaws and all.
outcomes.log: The ledger was sealed. The frailties will pass on, and so will the founders' shape of a human being.

title: The Frozen Debt
description: A prisoner condemned before departure still sleeps in cryo, sentence unserved, crime almost forgotten. The pod is failing, and a decision can no longer be deferred.

outcomes.label: Wake and pardon
outcomes.description: The crime belongs to a dead world.
outcomes.log: The prisoner woke to a ship that had never known their name. The old sentence died with the world that passed it.

outcomes.label: Let the pod fail
outcomes.description: The sentence was lawful. Let it stand.
outcomes.log: The pod was left to its failure. The law of a lost world was kept, coldly, to the letter.

title: The Triage Rule
description: Care is finite and the ship is not young. The council is asked to write down, once and for all, who the medics save first when they cannot save everyone.

outcomes.label: The young first
outcomes.description: The future outweighs the past.
outcomes.log: The rule was written: the young before the old. The elders signed it themselves, and said nothing.

outcomes.label: The needed first
outcomes.description: The ship keeps those it cannot run without.
outcomes.log: The rule named the skilled as first to be saved. It is efficient. No one is proud of it.

title: The Garden World
description: A living world drifts across the route — blue water, breathable air, empty of people. It could refill the holds for a decade. Some aboard have already stopped calling it a stop.

outcomes.label: Resupply and go
outcomes.description: Fill the holds; keep the schedule.
outcomes.log: The ship gorged the world's bounty and pulled away before the temptation could set. The holds are full; the longing is not.

outcomes.label: Let the willing stay
outcomes.description: A world like this asks to be lived on.
outcomes.log: A whole people chose the garden world over the dark. The ship sailed lighter, and grieved the leaving even as it filled its holds.

title: The Skim
description: A gas giant sits astride the lane, its upper atmosphere thick with reaction mass. A skimming pass could top the tanks — if the hull can take the heat.

outcomes.label: Skim deep
outcomes.description: Dip low and fill the tanks.
outcomes.log: The ship dipped into the storm-lit clouds and came out streaked with soot and heavy with fuel.

outcomes.label: Skim shallow
outcomes.description: A cautious pass; take what's safe.
outcomes.log: A gentle skim gave the tanks a modest gift and the hull no new scars.

title: The Blight
description: A blight has taken the on-station crop and the seed vault with it. The mission's whole purpose here is rotting in the trays, and it will not come back this trip.

outcomes.label: Scour and replant
outcomes.description: Burn it out and try once more.
outcomes.log: The trays were scoured to bare metal and sown again from the last clean stock. It cost dearly, and it might yet hold.

outcomes.label: Cut losses and turn back
outcomes.description: There is nothing left to work for here.
outcomes.log: The blighted decks were sealed and the mission cut short. The ship came about, holds light and hopes lighter, for the long road home.

title: The Ghost Signal
description: A signal repeats on a dead channel — the ship's own call sign, in the ship's own voice, timestamped for a year that has not happened yet.

outcomes.label: Answer it
outcomes.description: Speak back into the dark and see what speaks.
outcomes.log: The ship answered its own ghost. The channel went silent, and stayed silent, and no one aboard has slept quite the same.

outcomes.label: Kill the channel
outcomes.description: Some frequencies are best left dead.
outcomes.log: The channel was shut and welded. Whatever was calling, the ship stopped listening.

title: The Empty Suit
description: A routine spacewalk finds a hardsuit tethered to the hull — sealed, powered, and empty. The manifest lists no missing suit, and no missing soul.

outcomes.label: Bring it aboard
outcomes.description: Whatever it is, it belongs to us now.
outcomes.log: The empty suit was brought inside and logged. Its serial number matches no record the ship has ever kept.

outcomes.label: Cut it loose
outcomes.description: Let the dark keep its own puzzles.
outcomes.log: The suit was cut from the tether and let drift. It fell away slowly, arms open, as if waving.

title: The Hum
description: A vibration has entered the hull — low, steady, and coming from nowhere the engineers can name. It is not dangerous. It is only always there.

outcomes.label: Chase it down
outcomes.description: Tear open the decks until it's found.
outcomes.log: The decks were opened and closed and opened again. The hum was never found — but the crew stopped hearing it, in time.

title: The Long Sleep
description: A somnolence is spreading deck by deck — the afflicted sleep sixteen hours, then twenty, then do not fully wake. The medics have no name for it yet.

outcomes.label: Quarantine the afflicted
outcomes.description: Seal them off and study the sickness cold.
outcomes.log: The sleepers were sealed away and studied. The spread slowed; the ship grew quieter, ward by ward.

outcomes.label: Push through on stimulants
outcomes.description: Keep the ship awake by force.
outcomes.log: The crew was driven awake with drugs and discipline. The work continued, hollow-eyed, on borrowed alertness.

title: The Quiet Fever
description: A slow fever runs the corridors — never fatal, never quite gone, sapping the strength of a generation raised on recycled everything.

outcomes.label: Reseed the ship's biome
outcomes.description: Rebuild the gut-flora from the vault.
outcomes.log: The living stores of the ship were reseeded from the founders' vault. The fever broke, slowly, and colour returned to the decks.

outcomes.label: Wait it out
outcomes.description: The body will learn the ship, given time.
outcomes.log: The council chose patience. The fever lingered a generation, and the survivors carried its mark.

title: The Lodestar
description: The scouts find it on the outbound leg: a derelict archive-ship, holds heavy with charts and cold-stored fortune, drifting exactly where no chart said to look.

outcomes.label: Log it and press on
outcomes.description: The charter comes first; mark it for the ships that follow.
outcomes.log: The lodestar was logged, sampled, and left for the ships that would come after. The charter still called, and the ship answered.

outcomes.label: Claim it and turn home
outcomes.description: A find this rich is a mission of its own.
outcomes.log: The lodestar's holds gave up a fortune in charts and metal. With wealth like this aboard, the council saw no reason to sail farther — the ship came about for home, heavy and triumphant.

title: The Mission Becomes Creed
description: A generation that has never seen a planet has begun to worship the voyage itself — the charter read as scripture, the founders as saints, the destination as a promised land.

outcomes.label: Let the faith carry them
outcomes.description: A ship that believes is a ship that endures.
outcomes.log: The council let the new faith grow. The crew sails on certain of its purpose, and a little further from the people who wrote the charter as a work order.

outcomes.label: Temper it with the record
outcomes.description: Keep the charter a charter, not a gospel.
outcomes.log: The council read the charter aloud as the plain contract it was. Some were steadied; some felt something sacred quietly taken from them.

title: The Silence From Home
description: The long-range relays have gone quiet. Not jammed — quiet. The civilization that launched this ship has stopped answering, and no one aboard remembers its voice well enough to say when.

outcomes.label: Mourn, and go on
outcomes.description: Whatever waits at the end, we are its people now.
outcomes.log: The ship held a wake for a home it could no longer hear. The mission stopped being an errand for someone else and became, simply, the ship's own life.

outcomes.label: Keep the watch
outcomes.description: Someone must still be listening. We keep calling.
outcomes.log: The relays were kept open, calling into a silence that never answered. It is a kind of loyalty, and a kind of grief.

title: The Long Schism
description: The decks that keep different calendars now keep different laws. What began as a cultural drift has hardened into two peoples sharing one hull, and one of them is packing.

outcomes.label: Force the union
outcomes.description: One ship, one law — enforced.
outcomes.log: The council imposed a single law by force of the security decks. The union held. The resentment went underground and stayed there.

outcomes.label: Let the dissenters go
outcomes.description: Better a clean parting than a war in the corridors.
outcomes.log: The dissenting faction took a lifeboat and the ship's blessing and struck out on their own heading. The hull is quieter, and smaller.

title: The Sealed Ward
description: A wasting sickness takes hold in the aft medical deck. Contain it behind a bulkhead, or treat it in the open and gamble the rest of the ship.

outcomes.label: Seal the ward
outcomes.description: Weld the bulkhead. Those inside are on their own.
outcomes.log: The aft ward went dark behind a welded door. The sickness died with those it had reached, and the ship remembered the sound of the welding torch.

outcomes.label: Treat it in the open
outcomes.description: No walls. The physicians work the wards and hope.
outcomes.log: The physicians refused to seal anyone away. More died — but no deck was ever haunted by a locked door.

title: The Ward Reopens
description: Generations on, a maintenance crew cuts into a long-welded aft bulkhead and finds the sealed ward exactly as it was left — and the ship must decide what that door means now.

outcomes.label: Make it a memorial
outcomes.description: Leave the ward as a shrine to the ancestors who were given up.
outcomes.log: The old ward became a place of pilgrimage. The descendants forgave the door by naming it holy.

outcomes.label: Strip it for the living
outcomes.description: The dead do not need the space or the salvage. The living do.
outcomes.log: The ward was gutted for parts. The ship gained a workshop and lost a grave, and not everyone agreed which mattered more.

title: The Tithe Ships
description: A local power intercepts the voyage and names a price for safe passage through their reach of space.

outcomes.label: Pay the tithe
outcomes.description: Credits and cargo are cheaper than a fight.
outcomes.log: The tithe ships took their cut and waved the voyage through. Word travelled that this hull pays.

outcomes.label: Refuse and run
outcomes.description: Burn hard past them and take the hits.
outcomes.log: The ship ran the gauntlet and wore the scars proudly. The tithe ships logged this hull as trouble.

title: The Rationing Referendum
description: The stores are thin and the decks want a say in who eats. Impose the cuts from the council, or put rationing to the whole ship.

outcomes.label: Impose the cuts
outcomes.description: The council decides. Order over consent.
outcomes.log: The council rationed by decree. The stores held — but a whole quarter of the ship stopped calling the mission theirs, and one day a berth-block was simply empty.

outcomes.label: Put it to a vote
outcomes.description: Let the ship ration itself, unevenly and loudly.
outcomes.log: The ship voted on its own hunger. The math was worse and the peace was better.

title: The Slow Pocket
description: Sensors find a fold of deep-slowed time across the course. Cut through it to save fuel, or route the long way around at cost.

outcomes.label: Cut through the fold
outcomes.description: Save the fuel. Let the years outside run strange.
outcomes.log: The ship slid through the slow pocket. To the crew it was a week; the star-charts outside had aged a decade, and the people came out subtly changed.

outcomes.label: Route around it
outcomes.description: Burn the extra fuel for a clean, ordinary passage.
outcomes.log: The navigators gave the fold a wide berth. The fuel gauges dropped and the calendar stayed honest.

title: The Understudy
description: The ship's ceremonial First Voice has died, and the only trained understudy is a nine-year-old who has memorized every word and refuses to be replaced.

outcomes.label: Let the child serve
outcomes.description: A child First Voice. The elders will cope.
outcomes.log: A nine-year-old read the founding words in a piping voice, and half the ship wept and the other half grinned. The role was never quite solemn again.

outcomes.label: Appoint a proper elder
outcomes.description: Dignity restored, the child heartbroken.
outcomes.log: A grave elder took the First Voice, and a small understudy was told to wait forty years for a turn.

title: The Cold Berth
description: A cabin appears on no manifest and in no memory — sealed from the inside, its logs blank, its occupant a name that matches none of the founding rolls.

outcomes.label: Open it
outcomes.description: Cut the seal and face whatever kept its own counsel.
outcomes.log: The cold berth held a workshop of impossible tidiness and a single stowed component, still crated. Who packed it, and when, the ship never learned.

outcomes.label: Leave it sealed
outcomes.description: Some doors are load-bearing. Weld it and move on.
outcomes.log: The council re-welded the cold berth and struck it from the tour. The ship agreed, a little too quickly, never to speak of it.

title: Boarders on the Hull
description: Grapples ring against the outer plating in the dark between stars. Someone is trying to come aboard uninvited.

outcomes.label: Repel the boarders
outcomes.description: Meet them at the locks. Hold the ship.
outcomes.log: The security decks met the boarders at the airlocks and threw them back into the dark. The ship held, and remembered who held it.

outcomes.label: Open a channel
outcomes.description: Buy passage with cargo instead of blood.
outcomes.log: A price was named and paid through the locks. No one died, and no one felt safe again on that stretch.

title: The Green Temptation
description: The charter's route skims a living world, warm and unclaimed. A faction petitions to be let off here rather than sail on.

outcomes.label: Let them settle
outcomes.description: Fewer mouths, fewer hands, a colony you did not plan.
outcomes.log: A whole faction went down to the green world with seed and tools, and the ship sailed on lighter, quieter, and short a people.

outcomes.label: Hold the course
outcomes.description: The charter comes first. Sail past the temptation.
outcomes.log: The council refused the green world. The charter held, and for a generation people spoke of the paradise they were not allowed to keep.

title: The Starless Reach
description: For a long stretch the charts show nothing at all — no star near enough to name, no world to steer by. A generation is born and dies seeing only the ship.

outcomes.label: Turn the ship inward
outcomes.description: Make the voyage itself the world. Festivals, archives, invented seasons.
outcomes.log: With no star to steer by, the ship became its own country — new holidays, new songs, a people who called the void home.

outcomes.label: Hold to the mission
outcomes.description: Drill the charter into them. The destination is the meaning.
outcomes.log: The elders held the charter up like a lantern through the starless reach. The people kept faith, and kept their eyes forward, and grew a little grim.

title: The Richer Find
description: The survey turns up something the charter never listed — a vein, a cache, a claim worth more than the whole contract. Log it and press on, or take it and turn for home.

outcomes.label: Take it and turn for home
outcomes.description: Cut the charter short. Sail back on the strength of the find.
outcomes.log: The find was worth more than the whole charter. The ship logged the site, filled its holds, and turned early for home, richer than the contract ever promised.

outcomes.label: Finish the charter first
outcomes.description: Honor the contract; the find will keep.
outcomes.log: The council marked the find and held to the charter. Debts are debts; the richer prize would wait for whoever came after.

title: The Rewriting
description: With the Ascension Circle ascendant, a proposal reaches the council: open the augmentation clinics to every child at birth, not merely the willing adult.

outcomes.label: Open the clinics
outcomes.description: Let the next generation be rewritten from the first breath.
outcomes.log: The clinics opened to the cradle. A generation grew up already something more than human, and already something other than their grandparents.

outcomes.label: Adults by consent only
outcomes.description: Hold the line at the age of choosing.
outcomes.log: The council held augmentation to the age of consent. The Circle called it timidity; the elders called it a boundary worth keeping.

title: The Tended Flame
description: The Keepers of the First Flame hold the ship's oldest reactor sacred. When it needs invasive repair, they ask that it be honored with ritual first — and the ritual takes time the fault may not allow.

outcomes.label: Honor the rite first
outcomes.description: Let the Keepers tend the flame their way. The engineers wait.
outcomes.log: The Keepers sang the reactor through its repair, and the work went slower and the ship held together in spirit if not in schedule.

outcomes.label: Send the engineers in now
outcomes.description: The fault won't wait for hymns.
outcomes.log: The engineers went in over the Keepers' objections. The reactor was saved and a sacrament was broken, and the First Flame did not forget.

title: The Living Decks
description: The Verdant Kin want to convert a cargo deck into gardens — more food, more air, more green — at the cost of the salvage bay the engineers rely on.

outcomes.label: Grow the gardens
outcomes.description: Trade the salvage bay for green.
outcomes.log: The cargo deck became a forest in miniature. The galley rejoiced and the machine shop mourned its lost bench space.

outcomes.label: Keep the salvage bay
outcomes.description: The ship runs on parts, not petals.
outcomes.log: The salvage bay stayed. The Kin tended window-boxes instead of a forest, and remembered being told no.

title: Flame and Ascension
description: The augmented and the traditionalists share too few corridors. The Ascension Circle and the Keepers of the First Flame petition, each, for a deck of their own — a soft partition of the ship.

outcomes.label: Grant the separate decks
outcomes.description: Let each keep its own quarter. Peace by distance.
outcomes.log: The ship drew a quiet border down its middle. The corridors grew calmer and the two halves grew stranger to each other.

outcomes.label: Refuse — one ship, shared decks
outcomes.description: No partitions. They will learn to live shoulder to shoulder.
outcomes.log: The council refused the partition and mixed the berth rolls on purpose. The friction stayed, and so, stubbornly, did the sense of one crew.

title: The Last Engineer
description: The one aging specialist who truly understands the reactor is failing, and no one has been trained to take her place. What she knows will die with her unless the ship acts now.

outcomes.label: Pull apprentices to her side
outcomes.description: Take hands off other work and sit them at her bench while there is time.
outcomes.log: For her last months the old engineer taught without rest, and three apprentices carried her diagrams like scripture. The knowledge survived the funeral.

outcomes.label: Let the work go on without her
outcomes.description: There is no one to spare. Hope the machines hold and the manuals suffice.
outcomes.log: The last engineer died with her understanding intact and untransferred. The reactor still ran — for now — tended by people who no longer knew quite why it worked.

title: Coolant Loop Rupture
description: A primary coolant loop lets go in the engineering bay, scalding two decks and warping the module's frame. It can be patched fast and dirty, or slow and right.

outcomes.label: Patch it fast
outcomes.description: Seal it and move on. The bay will carry the scar.
outcomes.log: The rupture was sealed within the hour and the bay never quite ran true again, its frame subtly bent, its tolerances forever a little off.

outcomes.label: Rebuild it properly
outcomes.description: Strip the loop and do it right, at real cost.
outcomes.log: The crew tore the coolant loop down to the frame and rebuilt it clean. It cost a fortune in parts and the bay came back nearly whole.

title: Rot in the Grow-Decks
description: A rot spreads through the hydroponic beds faster than the gardeners can cut it back. Burn the infected racks to save the rest, or nurse them and risk the whole harvest.

outcomes.label: Burn the infected racks
outcomes.description: Cut losses. Sacrifice a quarter to save three.
outcomes.log: The gardeners torched a quarter of the grow-decks and stopped the rot at the firebreak. The galley tightened its belt and the beds slowly greened again.

outcomes.label: Nurse the beds
outcomes.description: Try to save every rack. Gamble the harvest.
outcomes.log: The gardeners fought for every rack and lost most of them anyway. The rot burned through the decks and the ship went hungry into the next season.

title: The Forgotten Medicine
description: A physician opens the old pharmacopoeia and finds she can no longer read half of it — the training that made sense of it lapsed generations back. A fever season is coming.

outcomes.label: Rebuild the training
outcomes.description: Convene the physicians and relearn the lost art from first principles.
outcomes.log: The physicians spent a year relearning what their grandparents knew by heart. The pharmacopoeia opened again, and the fever season, when it came, was survivable.

outcomes.label: Trust the auto-clinics
outcomes.description: Let the automated systems prescribe blind. No one need understand why.
outcomes.log: The auto-clinics dosed by rote through the fever season and buried those they guessed wrong on. The understanding slipped a notch further out of living memory.

title: The Dry Tank
description: The reaction-mass gauges read near-empty with the destination star still a lifetime ahead. Something has to give, or the ship coasts dark.

outcomes.label: Cut the drives and coast
outcomes.description: Shut down all but life support and fall toward home on momentum alone.
outcomes.log: The drives went cold and the ship coasted through the dark on nothing but momentum and nerve. It cost years and it cost comfort, but it kept flying.

outcomes.label: Feed the reactor the ship itself
outcomes.description: Break down non-essential structure for reaction mass.
outcomes.log: The engineers fed whole bulkheads into the reactor. The tank climbed off empty and the ship came home lighter, barer, and scarred by its own hunger.

title: The Empty Lockers
description: The spare-parts lockers are bare and the ship still has centuries to run. The machinists want to set up a foundry to make their own — at a cost in metal and hands.

outcomes.label: Build the foundry
outcomes.description: Turn a hold into a machine shop and mine the cargo for feedstock.
outcomes.log: A cargo hold became a foundry, and the ship learned to feed its own repairs. The lockers filled again with parts stamped in the machinists' own dies.

outcomes.label: Ration what's left
outcomes.description: Hoard the last parts for true emergencies and let the rest wear.
outcomes.log: The last parts went under lock and key. Small faults went unfixed and compounded, and the ship carried a growing debt of deferred repair.

title: The Laden Return
description: The holds are heavy with everything the charter was worth, and a heavy ship is a slow, tempting one. Word of the cargo travels ahead on the homeward lane.

outcomes.label: Run dark and quiet
outcomes.description: Kill the beacons, burn fuel to stay ahead of trouble.
outcomes.log: The ship went dark and ran the homeward lane like a smuggler, holds full and running lamps cold. Nothing caught it, and no one slept easy.

outcomes.label: Hire an escort
outcomes.description: Spend part of the prize to guard the rest.
outcomes.log: A hired escort shadowed the ship home for a cut of the cargo. The prize came in smaller and whole, which the council judged a fair trade.

title: The Stowaway
description: A headcount turns up one person too many — a soul on no manifest, born in a maintenance crawlway to parents who hid a pregnancy from the census. By the ship's own law they were never meant to exist.

outcomes.label: Enroll them fully
outcomes.description: Tear up the census rule. A person is a person.
outcomes.log: The council struck the birth-quota from the code and enrolled the stowaway with a name and a berth. The old rule never recovered its authority.

outcomes.label: Uphold the quota
outcomes.description: The rule exists for the ship's survival. Make an example.
outcomes.log: The quota held and the stowaway was raised a ward of the ship, unpersoned in the ledgers. The decks did not forget the coldness of it.

title: The Mercy Dose
description: A wasting illness with no cure spreads among the eldest, and the physicians ask a question no one wants: should the dying be helped to go quickly, to spare both them and the stretched infirmary?

outcomes.label: Permit the mercy dose
outcomes.description: Let those who choose it end their suffering.
outcomes.log: The council permitted the mercy dose to those who asked for it. The infirmary emptied and something in the ship's conscience shifted, quietly, for good.

outcomes.label: Forbid it — care to the last
outcomes.description: No hastened deaths. Bear the cost of caring for every one.
outcomes.log: The council forbade the mercy dose and the wards ran full and grieving to the end. It cost the ship dearly and it kept a line the elders would not cross.

title: The Archive Lie
description: An archivist discovers the founding logs were doctored generations ago — the mission's origin was uglier than the story every child is taught. She asks whether the truth should be restored, or the kind lie left to hold.

outcomes.label: Restore the true record
outcomes.description: The people deserve their real history, however it lands.
outcomes.log: The archivist published the true founding, and a comfortable myth died in an afternoon. The ship was more honest and less certain of itself ever after.

outcomes.label: Let the kind lie stand
outcomes.description: Some fictions hold a people together. Seal the record.
outcomes.log: The council resealed the doctored logs and let the founding myth stand. The archivist kept her silence, and the ship kept its comfortable story.

title: The Mutineers' Sentence
description: A short, ugly mutiny is put down within a day. Now the ringleaders stand before the council, and the sentence set today will be remembered for generations.

outcomes.label: Exile them in a lifeboat
outcomes.description: Give them a shuttle and a heading and let the void judge them.
outcomes.log: The ringleaders were sealed into a lifeboat and cast off with a week of air and a prayer. The ship flew on, orderly and a little colder, and watched their light dwindle.

outcomes.label: Pardon them into service
outcomes.description: Bind them to the mission with mercy instead of fear.
outcomes.log: The council pardoned the mutineers and set them to the hardest, humblest work aboard. Most were broken by gratitude; the ship gambled on mercy and mostly won.

title: The Exiles' Return
description: Long after the mutiny that history half-forgot, sensors catch a battered craft matching the old lifeboat's transponder — and aboard it, the descendants of the exiled, who never forgot which ship left their forebears to die.

outcomes.label: Take them aboard
outcomes.description: Answer the old wrong with an open lock.
outcomes.log: The descendants of the exiled came aboard wary and armed with a century of grievance. The ship absorbed its own old sin, and grew stranger and larger for it.

outcomes.label: Turn them away again
outcomes.description: The lifeboat was a sentence. Let it stand.
outcomes.log: The council refused the exiles a second time, and the battered craft fell away astern. Some aboard called it justice; more of them could not meet each other's eyes.

title: The Second Star
description: A star that is not on any chart brightens dead ahead — young, wrong, and radiating on frequencies that make the physicists argue and the navigators nervous.

outcomes.label: Hold and study it
outcomes.description: Spend the time. A thing like this may never be seen again.
outcomes.log: The ship held station and drank the strange star in through every instrument. The data would feed the archives for centuries; the schedule never quite recovered the lost years.

outcomes.label: Give it a wide berth
outcomes.description: Whatever it is, it is not the mission. Steer clear.
outcomes.log: The navigators bent the course well around the wrong young star and its restless light. The physicists filed complaints; the captain filed them away.

title: The Stationkeepers
description: Parked at the worksite for lifetimes, a generation comes of age that has never seen the ship move. To them the mission is not a voyage but a place — this orbit, this labor, this sky that never changes.

outcomes.label: Make the work their identity
outcomes.description: Let them be stationkeepers, proud of the labor for its own sake.
outcomes.log: The council blessed the stationkeepers' pride, and a people who had forgotten they were travelers worked the vein like a homeland. Home would be a strange word to them later.

outcomes.label: Keep the voyage alive in them
outcomes.description: Drill the return into every child. This is a stop, not a home.
outcomes.log: The elders held the return before the stationkeepers like a promise, and taught children born to the worksite to ache for a home none of them had seen.

title: The Idle Hull
description: A ship parked for centuries suffers what a moving one never does — seals set, bearings seize, whole systems forget their purpose from disuse. The idle hull is quietly rotting at anchor.

outcomes.label: Run everything on a duty cycle
outcomes.description: Spend parts and hands keeping every system exercised.
outcomes.log: The engineers put the whole idle ship on a duty cycle, waking each system in turn so nothing seized. It cost parts and vigilance and it kept the hull from rotting at anchor.

outcomes.label: Let the unused systems sleep
outcomes.description: Power down what the camp doesn't need and accept the rot.
outcomes.log: The camp shut down every system it wasn't using, and the sleeping parts of the ship set and seized in the cold. Waking them later would be its own long ordeal.

title: The Covenant's Workshops
description: With the Steel Covenant ascendant, the machinists want half the education decks turned over to workshops — more apprentices at the bench, fewer at the books. Build now, they say; philosophize later.

outcomes.label: Give them the decks
outcomes.description: Workshops over classrooms. Let the ship become a forge.
outcomes.log: The Covenant took the education decks and rang them with hammers. The engineering bays had never run better; the archives and the classrooms gathered a generation's dust.

outcomes.label: Keep the classrooms
outcomes.description: A ship is more than its machine shops. Hold the balance.
outcomes.log: The council kept the classrooms open over the Covenant's grumbling. The apprentices split their days between bench and book, and nothing was built quite as fast.

title: The Middle Way
description: A quarrel between decks is curdling toward something worse, and the Meridian Accord — administrators to the marrow — propose a slow, unglamorous arbitration that will satisfy no one entirely and everyone a little.

outcomes.label: Let the Accord arbitrate
outcomes.description: Weeks of hearings, a compromise no one loves, peace that holds.
outcomes.log: The Accord convened its hearings and ground the quarrel down to a compromise as grey as it was durable. No one cheered; no one bled; the ship went on.

outcomes.label: Just rule from the chair
outcomes.description: Skip the process. Decide and move on.
outcomes.log: The captain cut through the Accord's process with a single ruling. It was faster and cleaner and it left a residue of resentment the administrators had meant to dissolve.

title: The Long Table
description: The Hearth Union petitions to make the shared evening meal sacrosanct — every soul at the long table, every night, no shift or duty excepted. Community over output, the remembered name over the quota.

outcomes.label: Set the long table
outcomes.description: Down tools each evening. The ship eats as one.
outcomes.log: The long table was set every night, and duty bent around supper. The ship lost some hours of labor and gained a century of belonging; the names were remembered at the table.

outcomes.label: Keep the shifts running
outcomes.description: The work does not stop for supper. Eat in rotation.
outcomes.log: The council kept the shifts turning through the dinner hour. The quotas held and the Hearth Union set a smaller table, and set it a little colder.

title: The Forge and the Hearth
description: The Steel Covenant wants to strip the old founders' commons — a beloved, inefficient garden-lounge — and replace it with a fabrication bay. The Hearth Union would sooner give up a lung.

outcomes.label: Raise the fabrication bay
outcomes.description: Function wins. The commons becomes a workshop.
outcomes.log: The founders' commons came down and a fabrication bay rose in its place. The ship made more and remembered less, and the Hearth Union mourned a garden as if it were a person.

outcomes.label: Keep the commons
outcomes.description: Some inefficiencies are load-bearing. The garden stays.
outcomes.log: The council spared the founders' commons, and the Covenant built its bay in a cramped hold instead. The garden kept growing, useless and green and beloved.

title: The Measured Ascent
description: The Ascension Circle pushes a sweeping new augmentation program; the Meridian Accord, wary of anything sweeping, demands it be throttled to a cautious trickle. Both factions look to the council to hold the pace.

outcomes.label: Throttle it to a trickle
outcomes.description: The Accord's caution wins; augment slowly, review often.
outcomes.log: The council throttled the augmentation program to the Accord's cautious pace. The Circle chafed; the physicians, given time, actually understood what they were doing.

outcomes.label: Let the program run
outcomes.description: The Circle's ambition wins; augment broadly and fast.
outcomes.log: The council let the Ascension Circle run its program at full tilt. A generation changed fast and unevenly, and the Accord filed its objections in triplicate, unread.

title: The Breath Keepers
description: The atmosphere plant runs on rote now — the last technicians who truly understood the scrubber chemistry are gone, and the current keepers follow checklists they cannot explain. A drift in the CO2 numbers has no one who knows why.

outcomes.label: Tear it down and relearn it
outcomes.description: Strip the plant to first principles and rebuild the understanding.
outcomes.log: The keepers pulled the whole atmosphere plant apart and learned it again from the metal up. The air thinned dangerously for a season, then came back cleaner than it had in generations.

outcomes.label: Trust the checklists
outcomes.description: Don't touch what you don't understand. Follow the steps and hope.
outcomes.log: The keepers followed the old checklists by faith and buried the ones the bad air took. The plant kept running, a black box no living soul could open.

title: The Unlearned Watch
description: Generations of peace have hollowed out the security corps — the watch stands its posts by tradition, drills half-remembered, nobody left who has trained for a real breach. A rowdy dispute nearly turns to blood before anyone knows how to stop it.

outcomes.label: Rebuild the corps properly
outcomes.description: Pull the old manuals, run real drills, restore the discipline.
outcomes.log: The watch drilled in earnest for the first time in living memory. The corridors felt safer and a little more guarded, and the corps remembered what it was for.

outcomes.label: Leave the watch ceremonial
outcomes.description: There has been no trouble in a century. Why break the peace to prepare for it?
outcomes.log: The watch kept its polished buttons and its empty drills. The ship felt calm and was not ready, and the knowledge of how to hold a breach slid another notch toward myth.

title: The Teachers' Gap
description: The schools have been coasting — teaching the shape of knowledge without its substance, graduating cohorts who can recite but not reason. One generation more of this and the ship forgets how to teach itself anything.

outcomes.label: Refound the schools
outcomes.description: Pull the best minds from every deck to teach, at a cost to the work.
outcomes.log: The council pulled its finest hands off the line and set them to teaching. Output dipped for a generation and the ship recovered its ability to make more of itself than it had been.

outcomes.label: Let the schools coast
outcomes.description: There is work to do. The children can learn the essentials on the job.
outcomes.log: The schools kept coasting and the cohorts kept reciting. The ship lost, quietly, a little more of its capacity to understand itself — a debt that would come due in some later crisis.

title: The Lost Gardeners
description: The grow-decks yield less every cycle, and the reason is quiet: the master gardeners' craft — the reading of soil and light and blight — was never fully passed on. The beds are tended by rote, and the harvests know it.

outcomes.label: Apprentice a new generation of gardeners
outcomes.description: Set the young to the beds under what few masters remain.
outcomes.log: The last master gardeners took apprentices to the beds and taught the reading of soil and rot. The next harvest was thin; the ones after that were not.

outcomes.label: Automate the beds instead
outcomes.description: Replace craft with machinery and standing recipes.
outcomes.log: The beds were handed to machines and fixed recipes, and the gardener's craft became a museum piece. The yields steadied — brittle, blind, and wholly dependent on the machinery holding.

title: The Hungry Wards
description: A run of poor harvests has left the ship not starving but malnourished, and the toll is landing in the infirmary: weakened immune systems, slow-healing injuries, a fragile population filling beds the medics cannot empty.

outcomes.label: Divert everything to nutrition
outcomes.description: Pour resources into food quality and let other work slide.
outcomes.log: The ship threw everything at the harvests and the ration quality climbed. The wards slowly emptied as the population regained its strength, and the deferred work waited its turn.

outcomes.label: Medicate through the shortage
outcomes.description: Prop up the sick with the infirmary's reserves and ride it out.
outcomes.log: The infirmary spent its reserves holding a malnourished ship together. The medics kept most of them alive, and the medical bay came out of the lean years worn thin and short on supply.

title: The Dimming
description: The reactor's output has sagged and the power budget no longer covers the whole ship. Something must be browned out — and the choice of what to starve of current says who the ship thinks it is.

outcomes.label: Brown out the habitats
outcomes.description: Keep the machines fed; let the living decks go dim and cold.
outcomes.log: The living decks went to half-light and the machine spaces stayed bright. The ship kept working and its people shivered through a long grey season.

outcomes.label: Brown out the workshops
outcomes.description: Keep the people warm; let production stall.
outcomes.log: The workshops went dark to keep the habitats lit. The people stayed warm and fed and the quotas fell to nothing until the reactor could be coaxed back.

title: The Ice Moon
description: A frozen moonlet drifts near the lane, its crust thick with the volatiles that make reaction mass. Stopping to mine it costs time and labor the charter did not budget for.

outcomes.label: Stop and mine the ice
outcomes.description: Spend the weeks. Fill the tanks off the frozen moon.
outcomes.log: The ship anchored to the ice moon and melted its crust into the tanks. The stop cost a season and bought a decade of range.

outcomes.label: Burn on past it
outcomes.description: The charter waits for no moon. Keep the schedule.
outcomes.log: The navigators logged the ice moon and did not slow for it. The tanks stayed low and the schedule stayed honest, and someone noted the coordinates for a leaner day.

title: The Seed Corn
description: The stores are thin enough that the granary masters raise a forbidden option: eat the seed stock — the reserve meant to replant the grow-decks — and buy this generation's bellies against the next generation's harvests.

outcomes.label: Eat the seed corn
outcomes.description: Feed the living now. Gamble the future can be rebuilt.
outcomes.log: The granaries opened the seed vaults and the ship ate its own future. Bellies filled; the master gardeners wept over emptied reserves that had crossed light-years unbroken.

outcomes.label: Hold the seed stock
outcomes.description: The reserve is sacred. Bear the hunger.
outcomes.log: The council held the seed vaults shut and let the lean season take who it took. The grow-decks would replant; the graves would be remembered as the price of it.

title: The Barren Decks
description: A generation after the seed vaults were emptied, the grow-decks still yield thin and wrong — the strains that crossed the stars are gone, replaced by whatever could be scraped together. The ship tastes the old decision at every meal.

outcomes.label: Trade dearly for new seed
outcomes.description: Spend whatever it takes to restock the vaults properly.
outcomes.log: The ship spent a fortune on new seed stock from a passing convoy, and the grow-decks slowly greened into something worth eating again. The lesson stayed in the ledgers, underlined.

outcomes.label: Make do with what grows
outcomes.description: Adapt to the poorer strains. Save the credits.
outcomes.log: The ship made peace with its thin harvests and forgot, over a generation, that food had ever tasted otherwise. The seed corn was never mentioned; the flavor of the decision simply became normal.

title: The Homeward Wreck
description: On the long leg home the ship passes a dead hulk, centuries derelict and picked at but not stripped. With the parts lockers thin, its bones are a temptation worth the risk of boarding.

outcomes.label: Board and strip it
outcomes.description: Send crews across to salvage what the hulk still holds.
outcomes.log: The salvage crews crossed to the dead hulk and came back laden — parts, metal, and a working component nobody expected. Two never came back at all; the ship does not ask what took them.

outcomes.label: Leave the dead alone
outcomes.description: Whatever killed it may still be aboard. Sail on.
outcomes.log: The ship gave the dead hulk a wide berth and its lockers stayed thin. Some called it superstition; the elders called it the reason they were still alive to argue about it.

title: The Wayfarers
description: Another ship hails across the dark — a generation vessel like our own, centuries into its own long errand, its people as strange and as familiar as a reflection. They propose an exchange of charts, craft, and stories.

outcomes.label: Open the exchange
outcomes.description: Trade knowledge freely with a fellow wanderer.
outcomes.log: For a month the two ships flew as one, trading charts and songs and the hard-won tricks of the long dark. Both sailed on richer, and a little changed by the meeting.

outcomes.label: Keep our distance
outcomes.description: Exchange pleasantries and nothing more. Trust is a luxury.
outcomes.log: The two ships exchanged careful greetings and careful silences, and parted with charts unshared. The elders called it prudence; the young called it a door left shut.

title: The Wary Frontier
description: The ship enters the claimed space of a civilization that has clearly been burned before — their hails are demands, their escorts are armed, their welcome is a warning. They want to know exactly what this vessel is and why it is here.

outcomes.label: Answer with full honesty
outcomes.description: Open the logs. Let them see the mission entire.
outcomes.log: The council opened the ship's records to the wary locals, mission and all. The escorts stood down by degrees, and safe passage was granted to a vessel that had nothing to hide.

outcomes.label: Give guarded answers
outcomes.description: Reveal only what safety requires. Keep the rest close.
outcomes.log: The ship gave the frontier only what it had to and slipped through under a cloud of suspicion. The locals logged this hull as one that keeps secrets, and passed the word ahead.

title: The Young World
description: The route passes a world of people who have not yet left their atmosphere — pre-industrial, unaware, gazing up at a new star that is really our drive-flare. To reveal ourselves would change their history forever; to hide is its own kind of choice.

outcomes.label: Reveal ourselves
outcomes.description: Descend, and become gods or teachers in their sky.
